Match found in: ./09/26/synapse-system/vendor/nix/dream2nix/lib/internal/nodejsUtils.nix
  identifyGitUrl = url:
    l.hasPrefix "git+" url
    || l.match ''^github:.*/.*#.*'' url != null;
  parseGitUrl = url: let
    githubMatch = l.match ''^github:(.*)/(.*)#(.*)$'' url;
      url = "https://github.com/${owner}/${repo}";
      splitUrlRev = l.splitString "#" url;
      rev = l.last splitUrlRev;
      urlOnly = l.head splitUrlRev;
      if l.hasPrefix "git+ssh://" urlOnly
        url = "https://${(l.last (l.splitString "@" url))}";
      else if l.hasPrefix "git+https://" urlOnly
        url = l.removePrefix "git+" urlOnly;
      else throw "Cannot parse git url: ${url}";

          url = "mirror://gnu/hello/hello-${version}.tar.gz";

  is_http_url = url:
    if (substring 0 8 url) == "https://" || (substring 0 7 url) == "http://" then true else false;
    if isString src && is_http_url src then (fetchTarball src) else src;

    pname = "urllib3";
    src = fetchurl {
      url = "https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/a7/c2/fe1e52489ae3122415c51f387e221dd0773709bad6c6cdaa599e8a2c5185/urllib3-2.5.0-py3-none-any.whl";
Match found in: ./09/26/synapse-system/vendor/nix/pip2nix/nix/sources.nix
        builtins_fetchurl { inherit (spec) url sha256; name = name'; }
        pkgs.fetchurl { inherit (spec) url sha256; name = name'; };
        builtins_fetchTarball { name = name'; inherit (spec) url sha256; }
        pkgs.fetchzip { name = name'; inherit (spec) url sha256; };
        ({ url = spec.repo; inherit (spec) rev; inherit ref; } // submoduleArg);
  fetch_builtin-url = name: throw
    ''[${name}] The niv type "builtin-url" will soon be deprecated. You should instead use `builtin = true`.
        import (builtins_fetchTarball { inherit (sources.nixpkgs) url sha256; }) { inherit system; };
    else if spec.type == "builtin-url" then fetch_builtin-url name
  builtins_fetchTarball = { url, name ? null, sha256 }@attrs:
        fetchTarball ({ inherit url; } // (optionalAttrs (name != null) { inherit name; }))
  # fetchurl version that is compatible between all the versions of Nix
  builtins_fetchurl = { url, name ? null, sha256 }@attrs:
      inherit (builtins) lessThan nixVersion fetchurl;
        fetchurl ({ inherit url; } // (optionalAttrs (name != null) { inherit name; }))
        fetchurl attrs;

  urlToPypiName = url:
    if builtins.isNull url then
        match = builtins.match "mirror://pypi/./([^/]+)/.*" url;
          "https://files.pythonhosted.org/packages/[^/]+/./([^/]+)/.*" url;
    (builtins.map (e: e // { pypiName = urlToPypiName e.src; }) sources);
    # Get the PyPI name of a derivation from its source URL. If it's not
        pypiName = if (builtins.isNull (urlToPypiName e.src)) then
          urlToPypiName e.src;

  parseGitURL =
    url:
      m1 = match "([^?]+)\\?([^#]+)#(.+)" url;
      m2 = match "([^#]+)#(.+)" url;
        url = elemAt m1 0;
            nameValuePair (unquoteURL (elemAt parts 0)) (unquoteURL (elemAt parts 1))
        fragment = unquoteURL (elemAt m1 2);
        url = elemAt m2 0;
        fragment = unquoteURL (elemAt m2 1);
      throw "Could not parse git url: ${url}";
  unquoteURL =
  # fetchurl doesn't un-escape the name, leaving percent encoding characters in the filename
  srcFilename = url: unquoteURL (baseNameOf url);

      isURL = source ? url;
        if isURL then
      gitURL = parseGitURL source.git;
              inherit (gitURL) url;
              rev = gitURL.fragment;

            passthru.url = source.path;

          fetchurl {
            url = package.source.url or package.sdist.url;
        else if isURL && format == "wheel" then
              whl: whl.url == source.url
            ) (throw "Wheel URL ${source.url} not found in list of wheels: ${package.wheels}") package.wheels;
          fetchurl {
            name = srcFilename wheel.url;
              url
            # Fetch wheel from URL
            if selectedWheel ? url then
                fetchurl {
                  name = srcFilename selectedWheel.url;
                  inherit (selectedWheel) url hash;
                lib.warn "wheel url '${selectedWheel.url}' missing hash, falling back to builtins.fetchurl" (

                    name = srcFilename selectedWheel.url;

            BLOCK_INFO=$(curl -s -X POST -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
        # Derivation to create a url2file locator script
        url2fileLocatorScript = { projectRoot }:
          pkgs.writeShellScriptBin "url2file-locate" ''
            GITHUB_URL="$1"
            if [ -z "$GITHUB_URL" ]; then
                echo "Error: GITHUB_URL is not set. Usage: url2file-locate \"https://github.com/owner/repo/blob/branch/path/to/file\"" >&2
            echo "Locating local file for URL: $GITHUB_URL..."
            OWNER=$(echo "$GITHUB_URL" | awk -F'/' '{print $4}')
            REPO=$(echo "$GITHUB_URL" | awk -F'/' '{print $5}')
            REPO_PATH=$(echo "$GITHUB_URL" | sed -E 's|https://github.com/[^/]+/[^/]+/blob/[^/]+/||')
          url2fileLocatorScript = url2fileLocatorScript { projectRoot = pickUpNix2.outPath; }; # Pass pickUpNix2.outPath as projectRoot
              curl # Add curl to devShell for testing
Match found in: ./09/crq-008.foaf.nix
  problemGoal = "While striving for pure functional systems (as outlined in CRQ-001 and CRQ-007), real-world agent operations often necessitate controlled side effects, such as accessing external services or securely managing credentials. Uncontrolled side effects can compromise reproducibility, security, and verifiability. Goal: To establish a secure, auditable, and reproducible framework for managing necessary side effects within agent operations, specifically focusing on secure credential management via a vault and controlled external communication via HTTPS URL access for single-step agent actions.";
  proposedSolution = "1.  **Secure Vault Integration:** Integrate a secure vault solution (e.g., HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager, or a Nix-based secrets management system) for storing and retrieving sensitive information required by agents (e.g., API keys, authentication tokens, private keys). Access to the vault will be strictly controlled, auditable, and integrated into the Nix environment to ensure reproducible access patterns. 2.  **Controlled HTTPS URL Access:** Implement a controlled mechanism for agents to perform single-step external network requests via HTTPS URLs. This mechanism will ensure that all external communications are explicitly declared, logged, and potentially sandboxed to limit their impact on the system's purity and reproducibility. Each external call will be treated as a distinct, auditable side effect. 3.  **Nix-Managed Side Effect Declarations:** Utilize Nix to declare and manage the dependencies and configurations for both vault access and HTTPS communication. This will ensure that the environment for executing side effects is reproducible and that all external interactions are explicitly defined within the Nix build. 4.  **Agent Step Isolation:** Design agent operations such that each step requiring a side effect (e.g., fetching a secret from the vault, making an API call) is isolated and clearly demarcated. This isolation facilitates testing, auditing, and understanding the impact of each side effect. 5.  **Auditable Side Effect Logs:** Implement comprehensive logging for all vault accesses and HTTPS communications, capturing details such as timestamps, agent identity, accessed resources, and data exchanged (where appropriate and secure). These logs will be integrated into the overall reproducible analysis pipeline (CRQ-001) for auditing and verification.";
Match found in: ./09/fetch-github-data.nix
      # Use curl to fetch repository data from GitHub API
          ${pkgs.curl}/bin/curl -s "https://api.github.com/repos/${owner}/${repoName}" > $out
Match found in: ./09/github-to-foaf.nix
  dctermsNs = "http://purl.org/dc/terms/";
      "@id" = repoJson.html_url; # Use repository URL as ID
      "${foafNs}homepage" = { "@id" = repoJson.homepage or repoJson.html_url; };
      "${foafNs}maker" = { "@id" = repoJson.owner.html_url; }; # Assuming owner is the maker
      "${githubNs}hasOwner" = { "@id" = repoJson.owner.html_url; };
      "@id" = userJson.html_url; # Use user URL as ID
      "${foafNs}homepage" = { "@id" = userJson.html_url; };

Match found in: ./09/nixify-resource.nix
  nixifyResource = { url, strategy }:
        ${pkgs.curl}/bin/curl -L -o $out "${url}"
        inherit url;
        inherit url;
        inherit url;

  # Conceptual function to resolve a cell's content if it's a URL or Nix node.
  # This would be an impure operation for URLs.
    if cell.type == "url" then
      # Conceptual: Fetch content from URL (impure)
        url = cell.content;
        nativeBuildInputs = [ pkgs.curl ];
        curl -L "${cell.content}" > $out/content
          resolvedContent = resolveCellContent cell; # Resolve URL/Nix node
            else if cell.type == "url" || cell.type == "nix-node" then
        (CellSchema // { id = "external_data_url"; type = "url"; content = "https://example.com/some_data.csv"; output = "// Fetched content from https://example.com/some_data.csv"; })

Match found in: ./10/01/docs/theory/url_provenance_mapper.nix
  # This function conceptually maps a URL to its verifiable provenance information.
  mapUrlToProvenance = url: 
      # 1. URL to Git Repo Mapping (Conceptual)
      # This part would parse the URL and use external services to find the Git repo and a specific commit.
        if lib.strings.hasPrefix "https://github.com/" url then
            pathParts = lib.strings.splitString "/" (lib.strings.removePrefix "https://github.com/" url);
            # For conceptual purposes, assume a fixed commit hash for any GitHub URL
            resolvedUrl = "https://github.com/${owner}/${repo}/commit/${commit}";
          { type = "unknown"; resolvedUrl = url; version = null; owner = null; repo = null; };
      originalUrl = url;
        gatewayUrl = if ipfsCid != null then "https://ipfs.io/ipfs/${ipfsCid}" else null;
        explorerUrl = if solanaContractAddress != null then "https://solscan.io/account/${solanaContractAddress}" else null;
  inherit mapUrlToProvenance;
Match found in: ./10/01/docs/theory/verifiable_file_topology.nix
      gatewayUrl = null; # IPFS Gateway URL (string)
      explorerUrl = null; # Solana Explorer URL (string)
      url = null; # URL in Git (string)
      gitUrl = null;
      gatewayUrl = if impureData.ipfsCid != null then "https://ipfs.io/ipfs/${impureData.ipfsCid}" else null;
      explorerUrl = if impureData.solanaContractAddress != null then "https://solscan.io/account/${impureData.solanaContractAddress}" else null;
      url = impureData.gitUrl;

  # Import the url_provenance_mapper for conceptual provenance tracking
  urlProvenanceMapperModule,
    specUrl, # The URL of the W3C specification (e.g., a GitHub repo, or a direct document link)
    name ? (builtins.baseNameOf specUrl),
    provenance ? urlProvenanceMapperModule.mapUrlToProvenance specUrl,
      inherit specUrl contentHash provenance;
      __impure = true; # Forking/fetching from external URL is impure
      echo "Conceptually forking and vendorizing W3C spec from ${specUrl}" >&2
      # 1. Using git clone --bare or similar for Git repos, or curl for documents.
      # 2. Storing the content in a reproducible way (e.g., pkgs.fetchgit, pkgs.fetchurl).
      echo "Vendorized content for ${specUrl}" > $out/${name}.txt

Match found in: ./10/10/nix2/lib/first-reflection.nix
          # Heuristic to find flake input URLs
          urlMatches = lib.strings.match "url = \"([^\"]*)\"" flakeContent;
          urls = lib.map (m: m.captures.c1) urlMatches;
        { inherit commands urls systems; };
      extractSubmoduleUrls = gitmodulesPath:
          urlMatches = lib.strings.match "url = ([^\n]*)" gitmodulesContent;
        lib.map (m: m.captures.c1) urlMatches;
      description = "Validation rules specific to Nix flakes, such as URL and system constraints.";
      validateFlakeUrls = urls:
          invalidUrls = lib.filter (url: ! (lib.strings.hasPrefix "github:meta-introspector" url)) urls;
          hasInvalidUrls = (lib.length invalidUrls) > 0;
          inherit invalidUrls hasInvalidUrls;
      validateUrlsWithPrefixes = { urls, allowedPrefixes }:
          invalidUrls = lib.filter (url: !(lib.any (prefix: lib.strings.hasPrefix prefix url) allowedPrefixes)) urls;
          hasInvalidUrls = (lib.length invalidUrls) > 0;
          inherit invalidUrls hasInvalidUrls;
        urlPrefixValidationReport,
        submoduleUrlUniquenessReport,
          URL Prefix Validation:
          Has Invalid URLs: ${if urlPrefixValidationReport.hasInvalidUrls then "Yes" else "No"}
          Invalid URLs: ${builtins.toJSON urlPrefixValidationReport.invalidUrls}
          Submodule URL Uniqueness:
          Has Duplicate Submodule URLs: ${if submoduleUrlUniquenessReport.hasDuplicates then "Yes" else "No"}
          Duplicate Submodule URLs: ${builtins.toJSON submoduleUrlUniquenessReport.duplicates}
Match found in: ./10/10/nix2/flake-checker.nix
{ lib, pkgs, firstReflection, urlExtractor, flakePaths, gitmodulesPaths }:
    inherit lib pkgs firstReflection urlExtractor;
  inherit (task) commandUniquenessReport urlPrefixValidationReport systemValidationReport submoduleUrlUniquenessReport submoduleBranchValidationReport;

    get-nix-file-list.url = "github:meta-introspector/get-nix-file-list?ref=feature/CRQ-016-nixify";
            urlExtractor = {}; # Placeholder
Match found in: ./10/10/nix2/task.nix
{ lib, pkgs, firstReflection, urlExtractor, monsterCode }:
  # Step 2: Extract all relevant information (commands, URLs, systems, etc.)
    flakeUrls = lib.flatten (lib.map (flakePath: (firstReflection.identityPrincipleSpec.rawCommandExtraction.extractFlakeCommands flakePath).urls) allFlakeNixFiles);
    submoduleUrls = lib.flatten (lib.map (gitmodulesPath: firstReflection.identityPrincipleSpec.rawCommandExtraction.extractSubmoduleUrls gitmodulesPath) allGitmodulesFiles);
  allUrls = extractedInfo.flakeUrls ++ extractedInfo.submoduleUrls;
  urlPrefixValidationReport = firstReflection.identityPrincipleSpec.flakeValidation.validateUrlsWithPrefixes {
    urls = allUrls;
  submoduleUrlUniquenessReport = firstReflection.identityPrincipleSpec.uniquenessValidation.check (lib.listToAttrs (lib.mapWithIndex (i: url: { name = "submodule-url-${toString i}"; value = url; }) extractedInfo.submoduleUrls));
    URL Prefix Validation:
    Has Invalid URLs: ${if urlPrefixValidationReport.hasInvalidUrls then "Yes" else "No"}
    Invalid URLs: ${builtins.toJSON urlPrefixValidationReport.invalidUrls}
    Submodule URL Uniqueness:
    Has Duplicate Submodule URLs: ${if submoduleUrlUniquenessReport.hasDuplicates then "Yes" else "No"}
    Duplicate Submodule URLs: ${builtins.toJSON submoduleUrlUniquenessReport.duplicates}
  inherit commandUniquenessReport urlPrefixValidationReport systemValidationReport submoduleUrlUniquenessReport submoduleBranchValidationReport;

{ lib, pkgs, firstReflection, commandUniquenessReport, urlPrefixValidationReport, systemValidationReport, submoduleUrlUniquenessReport, submoduleBranchValidationReport }:
    URL Prefix Validation:
    Has Invalid URLs: ${if urlPrefixValidationReport.hasInvalidUrls then "Yes" else "No"}
    Invalid URLs: ${builtins.toJSON urlPrefixValidationReport.invalidUrls}
    Submodule URL Uniqueness:
    Has Duplicate Submodule URLs: ${if submoduleUrlUniquenessReport.hasDuplicates then "Yes" else "No"}

Match found in: ./10/10/nix2/qa/test-prelude.nix
    # firstReflection and urlExtractor are implicitly passed through prelude.nix's arguments
    urlExtractor = import ../nix-url-extractor.nix;
  # Test 1: Ensure parsedUrls is not empty
  testParsedUrlsNotEmpty = lib.assertMsg (lib.length prelude > 0) "Parsed URLs list should not be empty.";
  # Test 2: Ensure each parsed URL has expected components
  testParsedUrlComponents = lib.map (parsedUrl:
    lib.assertMsg (parsedUrl ? owner && parsedUrl ? repo && parsedUrl ? url)
      "Each parsed URL should have 'owner', 'repo', and 'url' components."
  testOwnerIsAllowed = lib.map (parsedUrl:
    lib.assertMsg (parsedUrl.owner == "meta-introspector")
      externalRepos = lib.filter (parsedUrl: parsedUrl.owner != "meta-introspector") prelude;
      metaIntrospectorRepos = lib.filter (parsedUrl: parsedUrl.owner == "meta-introspector") prelude;
        lib.assertMsg hasFork "External repo '${externalRepo.url}' must have a corresponding 'meta-introspector' fork."
      metaIntrospectorRepos = lib.filter (parsedUrl: parsedUrl.owner == "meta-introspector") prelude;
          "Meta-introspector fork '${miRepo.url}' at '${expectedPath}' must be checked out and on branch '${currentBranch}'."
    inherit testParsedUrlsNotEmpty testParsedUrlComponents testOwnerIsAllowed testExternalReposHaveMetaIntrospectorForks testMetaIntrospectorForksAreCheckedOutAndBranched;
  allTestsPass = testParsedUrlsNotEmpty && (lib.all (x: x) testParsedUrlComponents) && (lib.all (x: x) testOwnerIsAllowed) && testExternalReposHaveMetaIntrospectorForks && testMetaIntrospectorForksAreCheckedOutAndBranched;

Match found in: ./10/10/nix-url-extractor.nix
  # Extract all URLs from flakes
  allFlakeUrls = lib.flatten (
    lib.map (flakePath: (firstReflection.identityPrincipleSpec.rawCommandExtraction.extractFlakeCommands flakePath).urls) flakePaths
  # Extract all URLs from submodules
  allSubmoduleUrls = lib.flatten (
    lib.map (gitmodulesPath: firstReflection.identityPrincipleSpec.rawCommandExtraction.extractSubmoduleUrls gitmodulesPath) gitmodulesPaths
  # Combine and deduplicate all URLs
  allUniqueGithubUrls = lib.unique (allFlakeUrls ++ allSubmoduleUrls);
  # Function to parse a GitHub URL into its components
  parseGithubUrl = url:
      githubMatch = lib.strings.match "github:([^/]+)/([^?#]+)(\\?ref=([^&]+))?(&dir=(.*))?" url;
      httpsMatch = lib.strings.match "https://github.com/([^/]+)/([^/]+)/tree/([^/]+)/(.*)" url;
      inherit url;
      inherit url;
      owner = null; repo = null; ref = null; dir = null; inherit url;
  # Parsed URLs
  parsedUrls = lib.map parseGithubUrl allUniqueGithubUrls;
  generateRepoNixContent = { owner, repo, ref, dir, url }:
        # Original URL: ${url}
        url = "${url}"; # Store the original URL
  repoFileInstructions = lib.map (parsedUrl: {
    path = "10/10/github/${parsedUrl.owner}/${parsedUrl.repo}.nix"; # This path needs to be relative to the root of the project
    content = generateRepoNixContent parsedUrl;
  }) parsedUrls;
  urls = allUniqueGithubUrls;
  inherit parsedUrls repoFileInstructions;

  extractFlakeFeatures = { flakePath, extractedCommands, extractedUrls, extractedSystems }:
      inputs = extractedUrls; # Example: use extracted URLs as a feature

    path = "vendor/nix/nurl";

{ lib, pkgs, builtins, githubApiUrl, buildGraphQLQuery, ... }:
      nativeBuildInputs = [ pkgs.curl ]; # Use curl to make the HTTP request
      # Execute the query using curl
      curl -s -X POST \
        "${githubApiUrl}" > $out/response.json

Match found in: ./lib/url_reader.nix
  fetchImpureUrl = { url, name ? "fetched-url-content" }:
      inherit url;
      nativeBuildInputs = [ pkgs.curl ]; # Use curl to fetch the URL
      curl -L -o $out/content.html "${url}"
Match found in: ./lib/url_extractor.nix
  extractUrls = { path, name ? "extracted-urls" }:
      nativeBuildInputs = [ pkgs.gnugrep ]; # Use grep to find URLs
      extractUrlsScript = pkgs.writeShellScript "extract-urls" ''
        grep -r -o -E "http(s)?://[^\"'<>[:space:]]+" "$path" > "$out"/urls.txt || true
      bash $extractUrlsScript
